label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 7

extraction.one_line_summary: SOUPE Geoffrey won the sprint after his designated leadout VAN GESTEL Dries failed to position, out-kicking AULAR Orluis by centimeters.
tactical_patterns: AULAR Orluis attacking through middle traffic after chaos demonstrates Venezuelan national champions excel in improvised sprint positioning.
GROVES Kaden winning intermediate sprint 35km out suggests sprint teams are prioritizing bonus seconds over energy conservation flat stages.
VINGEGAARD Jonas contesting intermediate sprint bonus seconds on flat stage signals GC prioritization over traditional role conservation.
Final corner positioned 350m from line created positioning bottleneck that nullified traditional leadout trains favoring opportunistic lone riders.

rider_signals.SOUPE Geoffrey: positive: leadout rider winning when designated sprinter disappeared proves he possesses finishing speed typically suppressed by team role
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: failing to win third consecutive sprint despite taking intermediate sprint suggests either positioning vulnerability or fading form
rider_signals.VAN GESTEL Dries: negative: completely absent from final despite having dedicated leadout reveals either crash involvement or critical positioning failure
rider_signals.AULAR Orluis: positive: nearly stealing victory through improvised middle attack confirms elite bike handling and explosive power in chaos
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: collecting intermediate bonus seconds on flat stage indicates GC ambitions despite modest time gaps
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: early crash into cornfield requiring medical attention for knee suggests developing injury concern before mountain stages
rider_signals.ARENSMAN Thymen: negative: race-ending crash 5km from finish eliminates key mountain domestique before critical weekend climbing stages
